metadata.description: Chinese vocabulary for daily schedules, classroom activities, and descriptions

content.name: usually
content.side_f: I usually wake up at six. (lit. I usually six o'clock get-up.)
content.side_g: 平常 is an adverb meaning 'usually/ordinarily' and typically precedes the verb or time phrase it modifies. It contrasts with 今天/现在 to mark habitual vs. exceptional behavior, and is close in meaning to 通常 but more colloquial. Often paired with 不 as 平常不… to mean 'not usually.'

content.name: early
content.side_e: Nǐ jīntiān qǐ de hěn zǎo.
content.side_f: You got up very early today. (lit. You today rise DE very early.)
content.side_g: 早 functions as an adjective ('early') and also as a casual greeting (早！= 'morning!'). In 起得早/来得早 it follows V+得 as a descriptive complement. Compare with the time word 早上 (morning); 早 alone emphasizes the quality of earliness.

content.name: so_this
content.side_f: There's so much homework today! (lit. Today homework this-much much!)
content.side_g: 这么 is a demonstrative intensifier meaning 'so/this (much/late/etc.)' before an adjective or verb, expressing speaker surprise. Pair with 那么 for distal ('that/so'). In exclamations like 这么晚/这么多, it carries a tone of complaint or astonishment, unlike neutral 很.

content.name: late
content.side_e: Nǐ jīntiān qǐ de zhème wǎn!
content.side_f: You got up so late today! (lit. You today rise DE this-much late!)
content.side_g: 晚 means 'late' as an adjective and also 'evening' in 晚上. As a complement (V+得+晚) it describes the action's timing, e.g. 来得太晚了. Its antonym is 早; 太晚了 is a fixed phrase signaling regret or reproach.

content.name: morning
content.side_e: Zǎoshang wǒ zuò gōngkè, ránhòu shàng kè.
content.side_f: In the morning I do homework, then go to class. (lit. Morning I do homework, after-that attend class.)
content.side_g: 早上 is a time word (usually 5–9 a.m.) and goes before the verb without 在, e.g. 早上八点. Contrast with 上午 (later morning, ~9–12) and 早晨 (dawn, literary). No measure word needed, and it can stand alone as an adverbial.

content.name: homework_schoolwork
content.side_f: I do homework at night. (lit. I evening do homework.)
content.side_g: 功课 is a countable/mass noun for schoolwork assigned at school; the verb is 做功课 ('do homework'). It overlaps with 作业 (more formal/mainland-standard) — 功课 is slightly more colloquial and common in Taiwan.

content.name: everybody
content.side_f: Everybody is busy. (lit. Big-family all very busy.)
content.side_g: 大家 means 'everyone' and typically pairs with 都 for emphasis: 大家都… It can be a subject ('everyone') or vocative ('Hey everyone!'). Literally 'big family,' it feels inclusive and warm, unlike the more formal 所有人.

content.name: to_go_to_class
content.side_a: to go to a class; to start a class; to be in class
content.side_e: Dàjiā bā diǎn kāishǐ shàng kè.
content.side_f: Everybody starts class at eight. (lit. Everyone eight o'clock begin attend-class.)
content.side_g: 上课 is a verb-object compound ('ascend-class') meaning to attend, teach, or be in session. Because 课 is already the object, you cannot say 上课中文; use 上中文课 instead. Its opposite is 下课 ('class ends').

content.name: to_begin_start
content.side_f: Class begins at eight. (lit. Attend-class eight o'clock begin.)
content.side_g: 开始 can be a verb ('to begin') or noun ('the beginning'). As a verb it takes another verb phrase: 开始念课文 ('start reading the text'). Its opposite is 结束 ('to end'); 从…开始 is a fixed frame meaning 'starting from…'.

content.name: to_read_aloud
content.side_f: We first read the text aloud. (lit. We first read-aloud lesson-text.)
content.side_g: 念 specifically means reading out loud, chanting, or reciting — distinct from 看 (read silently) and 读 (read, study, both silent and aloud). 念书 idiomatically means 'to study/attend school.' Tone matters: 念 (4th tone) ≠ 年 (2nd, 'year').

content.name: text_of_lesson
content.side_f: This text is very difficult. (lit. This lesson-text very difficult.)
content.side_g: 课文 is a compound of 课 (lesson) + 文 (writing), referring specifically to the reading passage in a textbook. Measure word is 篇 (一篇课文). Distinguish from 课本 (textbook) and 文章 (article, more general).

content.name: sound_recording
content.side_f: We listen to recordings to study. (lit. We listen recording study.)
content.side_g: 录音 is both a noun ('a recording') and a verb-object ('to record,' literally 'record-sound'). As V-O, it splits: 录了一个音. For the recorded file/device, use 录音 as a noun with measure words 个/段. Related: 录像 (video recording).

content.name: to_study_learn
content.side_f: I study Chinese every day. (lit. I every-day study Chinese.)
content.side_g: 学习 ('study/learn') is slightly more formal than its near-synonym 学; 学 is preferred in short colloquial phrases (学中文 vs. 学习中文, both OK). 学习 can also be a noun ('studies/learning'), as in 学习很重要. It doesn't take 了 as easily as 学.

content.name: handsome
content.side_e: Nǐ hěn shuài, yě hěn kù.
content.side_f: You're very handsome and also very cool. (lit. You very handsome, also very cool.)
content.side_g: 帅 describes male attractiveness ('handsome/sharp-looking'); for women, use 漂亮 or 美. It can extend metaphorically to 'slick/stylish' actions (这球打得真帅). Colloquially, 帅哥 ('handsome guy') is a friendly address for any young man.

content.name: cool
content.side_f: This method is very cool. (lit. This method very cool.)
content.side_g: 酷 is a phonetic loan from English 'cool' and is strictly colloquial/trendy, used for things or people with an edgy, stylish vibe. Unlike 帅, it doesn't focus on looks; unlike 好, it carries attitude. Common in youth speech: 太酷了！
